title: Collections, Taxonomy, Ordering, Navigation, Feeds, and Sitemaps · Contract Workshop
summary: A civic-publishing pair incrementally builds reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications from original fixed content fixtures while retaining earlier Python, Git, HTML, CSS, URL, accessibility, security, determinism, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Select published documents by typed metadata, language, route, taxonomy, and time under deterministic query and ordering rules.
Normalize tag and category identity separately from labels, aliases, hierarchy, route, and language.
Build global, local, breadcrumb, previous-next, related, and multiple-way navigation from an acyclic visible route graph.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Collection Query
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop publishing team handles case 1118 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Select published documents by typed metadata, language, route, taxonomy, and time under deterministic query and ordering rules.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Collection Query.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select published documents by typed metadata, language, route, taxonomy, and time under deterministic query and ordering rules. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Collection Query, keep Collection Query connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Feed Sitemap
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-feed-sitemap-1v0gg0" that uses case 3080 to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Feed Sitemap.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Feed Sitemap into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Feed Sitemap, keep Feed Sitemap connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-feed-sitemap-1v0gg0
# Competency: ssg-feed-sitemap.
# Case 3080: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_feed_sitemap_1v0gg0(published: int = 13, navigable: int = 13, taxonomy_terms: int = 5, feed_entries: int = 13, sitemap_urls: int = 13) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1v0gg0 = "1v0gg0-0gg0v1-3080"
    assert evidence_variant_1v0gg0
    evidence_axes_1v0gg0 = (
        "reader1v0gg0",
        "source3080",
        "parse0gg0v1",
        "route1v0gg03080",
        "artifact30801v0gg0",
        "recovery0gg0v13080",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1v0gg0)) == 6
    if published < 1 or navigable < 0:
        return False, navigable, "publication-count"
    if navigable != published:
        return False, navigable, "orphan-route"
    if taxonomy_terms < 1:
        return False, taxonomy_terms, "taxonomy-shape"
    if feed_entries != published or sitemap_urls != published:
        return False, feed_entries, "derived-publication-drift"
    assert feed_entries == sitemap_urls == published
    return True, navigable, "reconciled-site-graph"
